skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.summary: Generates a handoff document that summarizes conversation context for a new agent session.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and filesystem patterns at SKILL.md:7. These are false positives. The skill instructs the agent to use the standard mktemp utility to generate a safe temporary file path for the handoff document. No user input is passed to the command, and no sensitive data is exfiltrated. The two 'weak cryptographic algorithm' blockers are spurious pattern matches on the YAML frontmatter line 'name: handoff' and the argument-hint line, not actual cryptography. No malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: External command invocation via mktemp
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run `mktemp -t handoff-XXXXXX.md` to generate a temporary file path. This is a standard, safe utility with no user-controlled arguments. The resulting path is read before writing, preventing accidental overwrites.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is hardcoded and safe. No injection vector exists because user input is not passed to the shell command.

low_findings.title: Temporary file creation
low_findings.description: The skill creates a temporary markdown file to store the handoff document. This is expected behavior for a handoff skill and does not expose sensitive data beyond what the user already chooses to include in the summary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File creation is the core functionality. The path is generated safely by mktemp, and the file is read before writing.

content.value_statement: Long agent sessions lose context when you start fresh. This skill compacts the current conversation into a concise handoff document so a new agent can pick up exactly where you left off.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the current conversation into a structured handoff document
Suggests relevant skills for the next agent session
Avoids duplicating content already captured in artifacts, PRDs, or plans
Tailors the handoff focus based on user-provided arguments
Saves the document to a uniquely named temporary markdown file
References existing artifacts by path or URL instead of repeating them
limitations: Requires the agent to have access to the conversation history to summarize accurately
Does not automatically sync the handoff file across devices or sessions
Quality of the summary depends on the complexity and length of the conversation
User must manually share the generated file with the next session

use_cases.title: End-of-day handoff for remote teams
use_cases.description: A developer finishes a coding session and generates a handoff document so a teammate in another timezone can continue the work seamlessly.

use_cases.title: Switching between AI tools
use_cases.description: A user switches from Claude Code to Codex and uses the handoff document to transfer project context without re-explaining everything.

use_cases.title: Long-running project checkpoints
use_cases.description: A product manager creates periodic handoff documents during a multi-day research phase to maintain continuity across fragmented sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: You are ending your session and want a general summary for the next agent.

prompt_templates.title: Focused handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the handoff skill with: 'Focus on the authentication refactor and remaining test failures.'
prompt_templates.scenario: You want the next session to prioritize a specific task or area.

prompt_templates.title: Handoff with skill recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the handoff skill with: 'Next session will implement the new API endpoints. Suggest relevant skills.'
prompt_templates.scenario: You want the next agent to know which skills might help with upcoming work.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed project checkpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the handoff skill with: 'Create a comprehensive checkpoint for the Q3 dashboard project, including decisions made, blockers, and next steps.'
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a thorough handoff for a complex, long-running project.

output_examples.input: Run the handoff skill with: 'Focus on the login flow redesign.'
output: The agent creates a file at /tmp/handoff-a1b2c3.md containing:
- Summary of current progress on the login flow redesign
- Key decisions made during the session
- References to relevant PRD and design files
- Suggested skills for the next session
- Specific next steps tailored to the login flow

output_examples.input: Run the handoff skill at the end of a debugging session.
output: The agent creates a concise markdown file summarizing:
- Bugs investigated and findings so far
- Remaining unknowns and recommended next debugging steps
- Links to relevant logs or code locations

best_practices: Provide a brief argument describing the next session focus to tailor the handoff document
Review the generated handoff document before ending the session to ensure accuracy
Store the handoff file in a shared location if another person will continue the work
anti_patterns: Using the handoff skill without specifying focus, resulting in an overly generic summary
Relying on the handoff document instead of proper project documentation like PRDs or ADRs
Generating handoffs for sessions with no meaningful progress, creating unnecessary files

faq.question: What is a handoff document?
faq.answer: A handoff document is a concise summary of the current conversation and work status, designed so a new agent or user can continue without re-explaining the context.

faq.question: Where is the handoff file saved?
faq.answer: The skill uses the mktemp utility to generate a safe temporary file path, typically in the system temporary directory, with a name like handoff-XXXXXX.md.

faq.question: Can I specify what the next session should focus on?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass a description as an argument when running the skill, and the handoff document will be tailored to that focus area.

faq.question: Does the handoff duplicate content from existing artifacts?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly avoids duplicating content already captured in PRDs, plans, ADRs, issues, commits, or diffs. It references them by path or URL instead.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Do I need to manually transfer the handoff file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates the file locally. You must share or move it to where the next session can access it.
